data.title: American Atomics upsizes private placement to $2M
data.description:
• American Atomics (GNEMF) said on Friday it has upsized its non-brokered private placement to $2M from $0.5M, offering up to 8M units at $0.25 each.
  • Each unit will comprise one common share and one-half warrant, with each whole warrant exercisable at $0.35 per share for 24 months after issuance.
  • The company said it intends to use the net proceeds to support its previously announced transaction with Big Indian Prospectors LLC, as well as for working capital and general corporate expenses.
  • Shares -5.72%.

data.title: FDA to review Savara resubmitted Molbreevi BLA
data.description:
• The U.S. FDA will review Savara's (SVRA) resubmitted BLA for Molbreevi (molgramostim inhalation solution) to treat the rare lung disease autoimmune pulmonary alveolar proteinosis.
  • The agency granted priority review with an action date of Aug. 22.
  • The candidate is a recombinant human granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor.
  • Savara said it would submit applications to the European Medicines Agency and the UK's Medicines and Healthcare Products Regulatory Agency by the end of Q1.
  • The company resubmitted the BLA in December. 
  • Shares are up ~4.5% in after-hours trading Friday.

data.title: Top 3 things to look out for on Monday
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages finished higher on Friday as investors digested the latest PCE report, which came in hotter than expected.

The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) gained 0.9%, while the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) advanced 0.6%. The blue-chip Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) rose 0.4% on the session. 

Now, here are some of the top things to look forward to on Monday:

  • Investors will be closely monitoring the evolving tariff landscape and broader geopolitical dynamics in the wake of the U.S. Supreme Court ruling on President Trump’s sweeping trade policies. 
  • Market participants are likely to assess not only the legal implications of the decision, but also how it may influence future trade negotiations, global supply chains, and overall risk sentiment in the weeks ahead. Market participants will also be focused on Dominion Energy’s upcoming quarterly results, looking for clarity on earnings stability, capital spending plans, and any updates to full-year guidance. 
  • Investors will be assessing whether rate base growth, regulated utility performance, and progress on infrastructure projects can reinforce confidence in the company’s longer-term outlook. Investors will be closely monitoring the latest release of the Dallas Fed Manufacturing Index for fresh insight into regional factory activity and broader industrial momentum. The survey’s readings on new orders, production, and employment will be scrutinized for signs of expansion or contraction in the manufacturing sector.

data.title: Founder Group receives Nasdaq notice over publicly held shares deficiency
data.description:
• SFounder Group (FGL) on Friday said that it received a notification from The Nasdaq Stock Market that it no longer meets the minimum 500,000 publicly held shares requirement under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(4) for the exchange.
  • The company said the notice has no immediate effect on the listing or trading of its Class A ordinary shares.
  • Founder Group has until April 3, 2026, to submit a plan to regain and sustain compliance with Nasdaq’s continued listing requirements.
  • FGL +0.09% after hours to $10.28.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Quantum Battery Metals amends financing to non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Quantum Battery Metals (CSE: QBAT) (OTC Pink: BRVVF) on Friday said that it amended its previously announced financing to an ordinary non-brokered private placement.
  • The offering will consist of a minimum of 1.75 million units at $0.29 per unit for maximum gross proceeds of up to $560,000.
  • Each unit will include one common share and one warrant exercisable at $0.40 per share for 12 months. 
  • The warrants will be subject to an acceleration provision if the company’s shares trade at a volume-weighted average price of at least $0.80 for five consecutive trading days on the Canadian Securities Exchange.
  • Quantum said it intends to use net proceeds for exploration activities and general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • The closing is expected on or about March 10, 2026, subject to regulatory approvals.
  • BRVVF closed +33.36% at $0.3202.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Electra upsizes at-the-market offering
data.description:
• Electra Battery Materials Corporation (ELBM:CA) said on Friday it has increased the size of its at-the-market equity program to allow for sales of up to $25 million of common shares.
  • The shares may be sold from time to time through H.C. Wainwright at prevailing market prices on the Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • The $25 million aggregate amount includes up to $5.5 million previously covered under a December 2025 prospectus supplement.
  • Electra said it intends to use net proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes, including expenditures related to commissioning its Ontario sulfate refinery.
  • ELBM -0.0% after hours to $0.838.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NaaS Technology receives Nasdaq notification
data.description:
• NaaS Technology (NASDAQ:NAAS) announced on Friday it received a notice from Nasdaq on Feb. 17 stating it is not in compliance with the $35M minimum market value requirement for continued listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market, as set forth in Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(b)(2).
  • Nasdaq calculates MVLS based on total shares outstanding multiplied by the closing bid price and said NaaS has 180 days, until Aug. 17, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • The notice also said that the company fails to meet other Nasdaq standards, including minimum $2.5 million stockholders’ equity and $500,000 net income requirements.
  • The company said it aims to regain compliance by Aug. 17, 2026, and expects its shares to continue trading on Nasdaq during the period.

data.title: Foresight to change ADS ratio to 1-for-3 effective February 26
data.description:
• Foresight Autonomous Holdings (FRSX) said on Friday it will change the ratio of its American Depositary Shares to ordinary shares from 1 ADS representing 30 ordinary shares to 1 ADS representing 90 ordinary shares, effective February 26, 2026.
  • As part of the change, holders will receive one new ADS for every three existing ADSs held. The company’s ordinary shares will not be affected.
  • Foresight said its ADSs will continue trading on Nasdaq under the symbol “FRSX” with a new CUSIP number.
  • The company said it expects the change to increase the ADS trading price proportionally and help maintain compliance with Nasdaq listing requirements.
  • FRSX -4.08% after hours to $0.8057.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SA Analyst says choppy trading masks a building of strength beneath the surface
data.description:
U.S. equities are poised to climb further into the second quarter, according to Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research, who argues that underlying sector rotation is masking building upside potential.

“All the U.S. equity indices closed green on the week,” King noted, emphasizing that recent gains come despite months of sideways, choppy trading in the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite. He attributed the consolidation to weakness in software stocks being offset by strength in other areas of the market. In his view, the software group is now under accumulation and could soon provide renewed leadership. 

King added that unless semiconductor shares experience a sharp downturn, broader indices are positioned to advance alongside a recovery in software. “We believe the market will be significantly higher by end Q2,” he said. 

On Friday, Wall Street extended its gains after investors digested a hotter-than-expected Personal Consumption Expenditures inflation report. The Nasdaq Composite rose 0.9%, while the S&P 500 gained 0.6% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average added 0.4%, closing out a broadly positive week for major benchmarks.

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Amicus Therapeutics Non-GAAP EPS of $0.31, revenue of $634M
data.description:
• Amicus Therapeutics press release (FOLD): FY Non-GAAP EPS of $0.31.
  • Revenue of $634M.
  • Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities increased to $293.5 million at December 31, 2025, as compared to $249.9 million at December 31, 2024.
